mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month. Higher policy rates reduce corporate profit margins and dampen risk appetite.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack signals an imminent economic slowdown. This deterioration in employment conditions reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate robust residential demand. This joint strength in the housing sector boosts construction activity and consumer confidence, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production signals a post-shock supply recovery. This rapid expansion in manufacturing and mining output improves corporate revenue expectations, pushing S&P 500 returns higher within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods following a positive INDPRO shock, or the mechanism fails to produce a positive Rank IC during identified recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.5% year-over-year, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy. Elevated inflation erodes real corporate profits and increases discount rates, causing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. This positive employment trend sustains equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real sales of existing homes demonstrates broad-based economic demand. This dual strength in macroeconomic output and housing activity confirms sustainable growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below its 2017 baseline of 100, it signals a contraction in manufacturing and mining output. This supply-side weakness reduces corporate revenues and triggers risk-off sentiment, leading to negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when INDPRO drops below 100, or the threshold signal fails to achieve a positive Rank IC during contraction phases.
